questions.question: What is light?
questions.answer: Light is a form of energy that helps us see objects.

questions.question: How do we see objects?
questions.answer: We see objects when light reflects from them into our eyes.

questions.question: What is reflection of light?
questions.answer: Reflection is the bouncing back of light from a surface.

questions.question: What is a mirror?
questions.answer: A mirror is a smooth surface that reflects light to form images.

questions.question: Name one type of mirror.
What type of image is formed by a plane mirror?
What is a concave mirror?
questions.answer: A concave mirror is a mirror that curves inward.

questions.question: What is a convex mirror?
questions.answer: A convex mirror is a mirror that bulges outward.

questions.question: What is a lens?
questions.answer: A lens is a transparent object that bends light.

questions.question: Name one type of lens.
What does a convex lens do to light?
What does a concave lens do to light?
What is refraction of light?
questions.answer: Refraction is the bending of light when it passes from one medium to another.

questions.question: Give one example of reflection in daily life.
questions.answer: Seeing your face in a mirror.

questions.question: Which mirror is used as a dressing mirror?
Which mirror is used as a rear-view mirror in vehicles?
Which mirror is used as a shaving mirror?
What instrument uses a convex lens to magnify objects?
Which lens is used to correct short-sightedness?
What is the law of reflection?
questions.answer: The angle of incidence equals the angle of reflection.

questions.question: Why do we see objects in daylight?
questions.answer: Because light reflects from objects into our eyes.

questions.question: What happens when light strikes a mirror?
Why do objects appear invisible in darkness?
questions.answer: Because no light reflects from them.

questions.question: Name one device that uses lenses.
Name one device that uses mirrors.
